gaps: 🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — Tab switch vs. screen exit gap: The bug is about saving Custom EQ and then reopening the EQ page (navigating away and back). Existing tests 65641575 (navigate away/back) and 65641574 (close/reopen EQ tab) test persistence, but they test preset selection persistence, NOT Custom EQ slider value persistence. The bug specifically involves custom slider adjustments being lost. Test 65641575 says 'EQ setting persists' but doesn't specify if it validates the actual custom slider positions or just the preset selection. This is a critical nuance — the bug is about the slider values within Custom EQ, not which preset is selected.

🧠 STATE TRIGGER — Save button behavior untested: The bug involves tapping the Save button (step 5) and then exiting/re-entering. None of the existing tests explicitly validate that the Save button actually persists the custom slider values to the device/app state. Test 65641568 only checks that Save is disabled until a slider is moved — it never validates that pressing Save actually works. There is no test that: (a) adjusts custom sliders, (b) taps Save, (c) exits the page, (d) reopens, and (e) verifies the sliders are at the saved positions.

🧠 DEVICE ASYMMETRY — No device-specific validation: The bug scenario doesn't specify a device, but the domain knowledge shows different Poly devices have different EQ capabilities (e.g., Voyager Free 60+, Surround 80, Sync 20/20+). None of the existing tests run the Custom EQ save-and-reopen scenario across multiple device types. A bug might exist on one device (e.g., Surround 85) but not another (e.g., Free 60), and this asymmetry is completely untested.

🧠 LIFECYCLE DIMENSION — App kill/relaunch with Custom EQ: Test 65641576 tests EQ persistence after app kill/relaunch but only for 'all EQ values (Bass, Flat, Bright, Custom EQ)'. If this test only checks that the Custom EQ preset is still selected (not the actual slider positions), it misses the bug. The bug is about the slider values within Custom EQ being reset to defaults after re-entering the page, which is a different failure mode than the preset selection being lost.

🧠 EDGE CASE — No test for rapid save-and-reopen: The bug steps involve a simple save, exit, reopen sequence. There is no test that validates the slider values immediately after saving and reopening (without any other intervening action like backgrounding or disconnect). This is the most basic persistence test and it's missing for Custom EQ slider values specifically.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_001: Custom EQ slider persistence after navigating away and back (Tab switch)
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Navigate away: Tap back arrow to Audio Settings
Observe the Custom EQ slider positions
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: All sliders show saved values (125Hz=+3, 250Hz=+1, 1kHz=0, 4kHz=-2, 8kHz=-4). Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to default flat (0dB) positions despite 'Custom EQ' preset still being selected.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_002: Custom EQ slider persistence after closing and reopening EQ tab (Screen exit)
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Exit EQ screen: Tap back arrow to Audio Settings
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Sliders at 125Hz=+6, 500Hz=+3, 2kHz=-1, 8kHz=-5. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults. This validates the exact bug scenario — slider values lost after save+exit+reopen.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_003: Immediate state trigger — Save and IMMEDIATELY kill app before navigating away
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
IMMEDIATELY force-kill the app (swipe from recents) — do NOT navigate away or switch tabs first
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Sliders persist at 125Hz=+5, 1kHz=-3, 8kHz=+2. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults. This validates that the Save button commits state immediately, not just on screen exit.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_004: Deep lifecycle — Custom EQ persistence after Bluetooth disconnect/reconnect
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Wait for app to re-establish connection
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Sliders persist at 125Hz=+4, 500Hz=+2, 2kHz=0, 8kHz=-3. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults after Bluetooth reconnection cycle.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_005: Deep lifecycle — Custom EQ persistence after device power off/on
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Sliders persist at 125Hz=-2, 1kHz=+4, 8kHz=+1. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults after device power cycle.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_006: Device asymmetry — Custom EQ persistence on Poly Sync 20+ (speakerphone)
steps: Prerequisite: Poly Sync 20+ connected via Bluetooth
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Sliders persist at 125Hz=+2, 500Hz=+1, 2kHz=-1, 8kHz=-2. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults. This test targets Sync 20+ specifically to catch device-asymmetric bugs where speakerphone EQ persistence differs from headset EQ persistence.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_007: Device asymmetry — Custom EQ persistence on Poly Surround 85
steps: Prerequisite: Poly Surround 85 connected via Bluetooth
Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Sliders persist at 125Hz=+7, 250Hz=+5, 1kHz=0, 4kHz=-3, 8kHz=-6. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults. This test targets Surround 85 specifically to catch device-asymmetric bugs.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_008: Rapid save-and-reopen — immediate re-entry without any intervening action
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Immediately tap back arrow to Audio Settings
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Sliders persist at 125Hz=+3, 2kHz=-2, 8kHz=+4. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults. This is the most basic persistence test — validates that save commits state synchronously before allowing re-entry.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_009: Multiple save cycles — save, exit, reopen, adjust again, save, exit, reopen
steps: Cycle 1: Navigate to Equalizer, select Custom EQ, set 125Hz=+3dB, 1kHz=0dB, 8kHz=-2dB, tap Save, exit, reopen — verify sliders at +3/0/-2
Cycle 2: Without leaving Custom EQ, adjust to 125Hz=+6dB, 1kHz=-1dB, 8kHz=-4dB, tap Save, exit, reopen — verify sliders at +6/-1/-4
Cycle 3: Adjust to 125Hz=0dB, 1kHz=+5dB, 8kHz=+2dB, tap Save, exit, reopen — verify sliders at 0/+5/+2
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Each cycle correctly persists the latest saved slider values. Actual (If Bug): Sliders revert to a previous save or to defaults after any cycle, indicating the save operation is not overwriting the persisted state correctly.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_010: Edge case — Save with all sliders at extreme values (max/min)
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Set all sliders to maximum (+10dB or highest allowed value)
Now set all sliders to minimum (-10dB or lowest allowed value)
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: All sliders persist at maximum after first save, and at minimum after second save. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults or to a different value, indicating boundary value persistence failure.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_011: App backgrounding (home button) during Custom EQ page with unsaved changes
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
Press Home button (app goes to background)
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: App returns to Equalizer page with sliders still showing 125Hz=+4, 500Hz=-1, 8kHz=+3 (unsaved changes preserved in UI). Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to last saved values or defaults, indicating the UI state is not preserved across backgrounding.

generated_testcases.title: CUSTOM_EQ_012: Phone rotation while on Custom EQ page with saved values
steps: Navigate to: Device → Audio Settings → Equalizer
generated_testcases.expected: Expected: Sliders persist at 125Hz=+5, 2kHz=-3, 8kHz=+1 after rotation. Actual (If Bug): Sliders reset to 0dB defaults due to Activity recreation losing the slider state.
